The origin of chocolate traces back to the ancient civilizations of Mesoamerica, specifically from the cacao tree (Theobroma cacao), which grows in regions near the equator. The Mayans and Aztecs were among the first to cultivate and use cacao beans to make a bitter drink called xocolatl.
